article_meta.title: | Updated November 25, 2025 9:00am EST Nearly 1 in 4 American households

reasoning: The summary is largely neutral, attributes findings to the Bank of America Institute, and avoids adopting loaded or sensational framing. Minor subjective phrasing (e.g., “most severe financial stress”) appears but does not significantly shift tone. Perspectives are represented proportionally to the article.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses neutral verbs and attributions; no loaded action words.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Key claims are tied to the report or quoted economist; future outlook is hedged (‘may continue’).
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Slightly subjective phrasing (‘most severe financial stress’) not directly quoted; otherwise factual.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: No dramatic or alarming language beyond the article’s content.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about unverified intentions or thoughts.
custom_reasoning.slant: Reflects the single-source analysis without unduly favoring an absent counter-view.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Captures the main data points and context presented.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Minimal; one phrase could be seen as subjective.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Tone aligns with the article’s focus without amplifying negativity.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Gives standard weight to institutional data without dismissing other views.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Maintains neutral framing and attributions throughout with minor exceptions.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Includes the report and economist perspectives; slightly over-inclusive (CEO/publisher) but not omissive.
